layers.name: Workspace
layers.description: Repository-level documentation, workspace manifests, local automation, and project guidance.

layers.name: Domain
layers.description: Business entities, invariants, typed domain errors, state transitions, and repository ports.

layers.name: Application CQRS
layers.description: Commands and queries that orchestrate use cases, transactions, ports, and domain decisions.

layers.name: Persistence Adapter
layers.description: SQLx repositories, SQL command/query files, schema setup, and seed data implementing domain ports.

layers.name: Server Bootstrap
layers.description: Composition root wiring database pools, adapters, use cases, auth, and transport dependencies.

layers.name: HTTP Transport
layers.description: Axum routers, handlers, DTO schemas, auth extraction, CORS, and protocol-specific error mapping.

layers.name: GraphQL Transport
layers.description: async-graphql schema, resolvers, auth, CORS, and GraphQL-specific response mapping.

layers.name: Auth Core
layers.description: JWT claims, validation, auth errors, and authentication service ports shared by transports.

tour.title: Project Rules And Intent
tour.description: Start with the README and AGENTS guide to understand the inward dependency rule and clean architecture constraints.

tour.title: Domain Model
tour.description: Inspect domain modules first because business invariants, enums, transitions, and repository ports define the stable core.

tour.title: Application Use Cases
tour.description: Review commands and queries to see how CQRS workflows load state, ask domain objects for decisions, write once, and avoid response hydration reads.

tour.title: Persistence Boundaries
tour.description: Follow repository adapters and SQL command/query files to see how abstract domain ports are implemented with narrow write results.

tour.title: Composition Root
tour.description: Inspect server bootstrap wiring to see where pools, repositories, services, and application use cases are assembled.

tour.title: Transport Adapters
tour.description: Compare HTTP and GraphQL transports; both should parse requests, call one command or query, and map responses without owning business workflow.

tour.title: Authentication And Database Setup
tour.description: Finish with auth boundaries and database setup to understand infrastructure concerns that remain outside the application core.
